sections.heading: Overall Grade Weightage
sections.content: The final module grade is calculated as follows: 40% from assignment average, 30% from the final proctored evaluation, 20% from live session participation and polls, and 10% from the capstone project. All components must be attempted to receive a module grade.

sections.heading: Passing Marks
sections.content: Students must score at least 50% in the assignment average and at least 40% in the final proctored evaluation separately. The overall module score must be at least 55% to pass. Failing either the assignment average or the final evaluation requires a re-attempt of that component only.

sections.heading: Certificate Eligibility
sections.content: The LearnBridge completion certificate is issued only when the student passes all modules in the program, maintains 75% attendance in each module, and completes the capstone project with a score of at least 60%. Certificate names match the legal name on the enrollment form.

sections.heading: Final Proctored Evaluation
sections.content: The final evaluation is a 90-minute proctored online exam with webcam and screen sharing enabled. Students get one scheduled attempt and one re-attempt within 14 days if they score below 40%. Use of external notes, second devices, or another person in the room leads to disqualification.

sections.heading: Re-evaluation Request
sections.content: Students may request re-evaluation of the final exam within 5 working days of result publication by paying a nominal review fee. Re-evaluation checks only for grading errors, not for re-solving questions. Assignment re-grading is not offered after the resubmission window closes.
